title: Series detail
surface: Threaded reading — v1 gold-standard, promoted in v2
summary: v1's series-detail surface is the strongest IA on the site (per brownfield diagnose F7-positive). v2 preserves the surface and promotes it to primary nav.

strategy.decision: Inherit v1 series-detail pattern unchanged (numbered, ordered cards with read-state). Promote to primary nav under Library or Reading group depending on D1. Apply the same numbered-card pattern to tag-detail and counterpoint-thread surfaces to extend its win.
strategy.why: Brownfield F7-positive identified series-detail as v1's strongest IA pattern — numbered reading-order cards that scale, with clear thread continuity. Per Rule 1, preserve what works. Per Rule 4 (one thing per surface), the series numbered-card pattern is exactly the right primary affordance for ordered content.
strategy.shipped: v1 series: numbered ordered cards, read-state markers, series-name persistence in nav. Working well per current-state captures.
strategy.gap: v2 keeps the surface as-is structurally; changes are limited to header framing (format badge for cross-surface consistency) and promotion in primary nav.
strategy.question: None blocking. Optional: extend the numbered-card pattern to tag-detail (currently broken at scale per F1) as part of D3 format-mix decisions.

breaksFromV1: header format badge for cross-surface consistency
promotion in primary nav (vs being hidden behind v1's 'More' menu)

currentState.summary: v1 series-detail: numbered ordered cards, clean type-scale, read-state markers. Strongest IA pattern on the site.
currentState.annotation: v2 preserves the pattern and promotes it. The series-card shape may also become the v2 tag-detail-page chunking pattern (D3).
